item.title: AstraZeneca Oncology AdComm
item.description:
• Summary: The FDA is preparing for an Oncology Advisory Committee meeting involving AstraZeneca. The meeting likely pertains to a regulatory decision for one of AstraZeneca's oncology assets, with the AdComm providing an independent assessment of clinical data before FDA action.
  • MoAs: N/A (specific drug not identified from articles)
  • Sources: Endpoints News

item.title: AstraZeneca Datroway Biomarker
item.description:
• Summary: AstraZeneca's Datroway (datopotamab deruxtecan) biomarker analysis reveals that the biomarker both gives and takes away—providing predictive value but also indicating limitations. Datroway is a TROP2-directed ADC under investigation for multiple solid tumors.
  • MoAs: Datroway (datopotamab deruxtecan): TROP2-targeting antibody conjugated with a topoisomerase I inhibitor payload (DXd)
  • Sources: Oncology Pipeline (ApexOnco)

item.title: Pfizer Elrexfio Multiple Myeloma
item.description:
• Summary: Pfizer reported positive Phase 3 results for Elrexfio (elranatamab) in second-line multiple myeloma, positioning it as a potential competitor to existing BCMA-directed therapies. The trial showed significant efficacy, though revenue expectations were tempered by market dynamics. Pfizer aims to leverage this data to expand Elrexfio's label and drive adoption in earlier treatment lines.
  • MoAs: Elrexfio (elranatamab): Bispecific T-cell engager (BiTE) targeting B-cell maturation antigen (BCMA) on myeloma cells and CD3 on T cells, inducing T-cell-mediated cytotoxicity.
  • Sources: Endpoints News, Fierce Pharma

item.title: GSK Oncology Pipeline
item.description:
• Summary: GSK is positioning its oncology pipeline as a key growth driver, with the CEO pointing to several potential blockbusters. The pipeline includes assets in immuno-oncology, antibody-drug conjugates, and targeted therapies. Investors are watching closely as GSK aims to revive its oncology franchise through both internal development and partnerships.
  • MoAs: N/A
  • Sources: Fierce Biotech

item.title: BeOne VEGF Multispecific
item.description:
• Summary: BeOne's VEGF multispecific antibody is being developed as a cost-effective alternative in the VEGF-targeting space. The 'bargain basement' approach aims to compete with established anti-VEGF therapies by offering a multispecific design that could address resistance mechanisms. Early data and development strategy are under review.
  • MoAs: VEGF multispecific antibody (targets VEGF and potentially other angiogenic factors)
  • Sources: ApexOnco
